data.title: ChipMOS sees 6.3% Y/Y revenue growth for 2024
data.description:
• ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES (NASDAQ:IMOS) expects revenue growth of around 6.3% year-on-year for full-year 2024, reflecting its “continued leadership in its core markets” even as fourth quarter revenue fell.
  • Q4 revenue was $164.7M, down -5.7% Y/Y and -11% from the third quarter of 2024. The company noted that the quarterly decline reflects macro industry pricing and demand headwinds.
  • December revenue fell 3% M/M and 5.4% Y/Y to $54.3M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Maple Leaf Foods raises dividend by 9%
data.description:
• Maple Leaf Foods (TSX:MFI:CA) declared CAD 0.24/share quarterly dividend, 9.1% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.22.
  • Payable March 31; for shareholders of record March 7; ex-div March 7.
  • See MFI:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Outlook remains bearish for European gas prices, BMI says
data.description:
European natural gas prices have rallied at the start of the year, reaching a peak of EUR50/MWh on the back of increased winter demand and fears over supply following the end of the Russia-Ukraine gas transit deal.

While analysts at BMI Research acknowledge significant upside risk to prices in 2025 from below average storage levels, European markets, according to them, are well-prepared to plug the gap with increased LNG shipments, supporting the bearish outlook for TTF (NGEU:COM) and NBP prices.

Henry Hub prices in contrast have softened in the first week of January, though they remain elevated compared to 2024. BMI Research expects Henry Hub prices (NG1:COM) to increase by 41% y-o-y, from $2.4mnbtu in 2024 to $3.4mnbtu in 2025.

"The key drivers behind elevated Henry Hub front month prices are the rise in European benchmarks and cold weather in key gas-consuming U.S. states boosting domestic prices."

On the other hand, weak demand in manufacturing and other industrial sectors in Europe is seen reducing demand for natural gas, exerting additional downward pressure on prices.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Other Commodity Key Views:

While Brent crude (CO1:COM) has started 2025 on a strong footing, and is on its way to clock a fourth-straight week of gains, BMI's current forecast sees prices averaging $76.0/bbl in 2025, down from an average of $79.9/bbl in 2024.

"However, there are considerable risks to this view stemming from the uncertainties surrounding President-elect Trump’s second term in office."

Turning to industrial metals, which saw a mixed performance over the past week amid an uncertain demand outlook in Mainland China, analysts expect significant volatility in 2025 on the back of anticipated U.S. dollar strength and Trump-led trade policy shifts.

Among precious metals, BMI is bearish towards gold (XAUUSD:CUR), and expects prices to average $2,500/oz in 2025.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Stock index futures higher as investors look to key jobs report
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the green on Friday, as investors braced for the key December jobs report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.70%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.29%.

U.S. markets were shut on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

The next test for markets will now be the U.S. jobs report for December, Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"Indeed, it’s worth noting that one of the catalysts for this week’s selloff was the upside surprise in the 'prices paid' component of the ISM services index on Tuesday. So another upside surprise in payrolls would give further momentum to the idea that the Fed should be cautious about cutting rates from here," Allen added.

Economists expect the U.S. economy will add 157K jobs to nonfarm payrolls in December, slowing from the initial November estimate of 227K. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate is expected to remain at 4.2%.

"The payrolls number is derived from a survey that has a 43% response rate, and that poor representation means significant revisions are much more likely. The market is looking for a fairly dull report overall—some moderation in job creation with a stable unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The January consumer sentiment is also expected to land during market hours, and is forecasted to come in at 74.

U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment is still vulnerable to partisan bias. The recent improvement is mainly due to surging Republican sentiment. Republicans are more emotional than Democrats, pushing the overall index higher. Partisan bias does affect the inflation expectations component, Donovan added.

data.title: Tesla runs software update to address recall of 239K vehicles
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has run an over-the-air software update as part of a recall of 239,382 vehicles by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).

The update was needed to address an issue with the computer circuit board that “may short, resulting in the loss of the rearview camera image."

The recall covered certain 2024-2025 Model 3, Model S, 2023-2025 Model X, and 2023-2025 Model Y vehicles that were equipped with certain car computers and operating a software release that was prior to 2024.44.25.3 or 2024.45.25.6.

As of Dec. 30, 2024, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) identified 887 warranty claims and 68 field reports related to the condition. The electric vehicle maker said it is not aware of any collisions, injuries or fatalities related to the condition.

Beginning Dec. 18, affected vehicles began receiving a software update that changes the vehicle power up sequence to prevent the shorting failure condition to the primary and/or secondary power component. No further action is necessary from owners of affected vehicles that are equipped with software release 2024.44.25.3, 2024.45.25.6 or a later release.

Tesla (TSLA) said it is actively working to identify which affected vehicles, if any, experienced stress that may lead to the shorting failure condition.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were marginally up in premarket trading.

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals announces CFO departure, Vincent Lau appointed successor
data.description:
• Wheaton Precious Metals (NYSE:WPM) said CFO Gary Brown will step down from the role, and Vice President of Finance Vincent Lau has been appointed a successor.
  • Brown's departure is effective March 31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BlackRock seeks to extend FDIC's deadline until March 31 - report
data.description:
• BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) seeks to extend the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation's deadline to reach an agreement related to the regulator's supervision until March 31, Reuters reported, citing a letter sent to regulators.
  • FDIC had set a deadline of January 10 for the asset manager to reach an agreement on how the regulator will oversee BLK's investments in FDIC-regulated banking institutions, the report noted.
  • Vanguard Investments had worked on a passivity agreement with the regulator in late December 2024, and the FDIC had asked BlackRock to sign a similar agreement by the Friday deadline, according to the report.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: MVST, RKLB, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday morning as investors awaited the release of both the Jobs Report and Consumer Sentiment data later in the day. These key economic indicators are closely watched, as the jobs report provides insight into employment trends, while consumer sentiment offers a gauge of consumer confidence and spending patterns, both of which influence market expectations for economic growth and potential policy decisions.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Shares of Microvast Holdings (NASDAQ:MVST) surged 44% following the company's announcement of a major milestone in the development of its True All-Solid-State Battery (ASSB) technology. This breakthrough represents a significant advancement in battery safety, energy density, and efficiency, offering solutions for applications like data center backup power systems and electric school buses while also paving the way for innovations in electric vehicles and robotics. Unlike conventional lithium-ion or semi-solid-state batteries, Microvast's ASSB eliminates liquid electrolytes and features a bipolar stacking architecture, which allows higher voltages unattainable by traditional battery designs. As Microvast progresses to pilot production, the company aims to revolutionize the battery industry with scalable, customizable energy storage solutions for high-demand sectors.
  • Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares rose more than 3% after the company inked an agreement with NASA to provide Neutron launch services through its existing VADR (Venture-Class Acquisition of Dedicated and Rideshare) contract. Neutron, Rocket Lab's new medium-lift reusable rocket, will expand the company's ability to deliver a wide range of missions, including CubeSats, Class D missions, and other payloads, across various orbits. Additionally, the Neutron rocket is positioned to be integrated into the U.S. Government’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Lane 1 program, a $5.6B IDIQ contract. Neutron is scheduled to debut with its first launch from Rocket Lab's Launch Complex 3 in Virginia in mid-2025.
  • Formerly known as Smart Global Holdings, Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ:PENG) shares soared 17% after the company revealed a strategic collaboration agreement with SK Telecom and SK hynix to develop and deliver comprehensive AI data center solutions. The agreement, signed at CES 2025 in Las Vegas, follows SKT's $200M strategic investment in Penguin Solutions, which was completed in December. This follows the company's upbeat FQ1 results.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's Q4 revenue beats estimates amid growing AI demand
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (NYSE:TSM) revenue for December 2024 surged 57.8% year-over-year to about NT$278.16B, amid a growing demand for AI applications.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) — saw revenue for the fourth quarter of 2024 grow about 39% year-over-year to around NT$868.46B. (The Q4 figures were based on calculations off monthly disclosures — October NT$314.24B, November NT$276.06B, December NT$278.16B).

The company's fourth quarter revenue of NT$868.46B, beat analysts' average estimate of NT$854.7B, according to a report from Bloomberg.

TSM's revenue for the full year 2024 jumped 33.9% to NT$2.894T, compared to 2023 when it generated NT$2.161T.

The Taiwan-based company's revenue for December 2024 rose 0.8% compared to November 2024.

The strong numbers from TSM strengthen expectations that big tech companies from Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) to Microsoft (MSFT) will continue to build and upgrade datacenters to boost AI development, the report added.

Earlier this week, Microsoft said it would invest $3B in India for cloud and AI infrastructure over the next two years. The tech giant has already unveiled its larger plan to spend $80B in data centers in fiscal 2025.

Over the past weekend, AI server maker Foxconn Technology —which is formally known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF) — reported 15.17% year-over-year growth in fourth quarter revenue to NT$2.132T, setting a record for the company's highest fourth quarter revenue ever.

TSM is slated to report its fourth quarter results on Jan. 16.

Shares of TSM rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Rupert Murdoch's Fox Corp. faces $2.7 billion lawsuit over Smartmatic defamation claim
data.description:
A New York state appeals court ruled on Thursday that Fox Corporation (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA), parent of Fox News Network, must face a $2.7 billion defamation lawsuit filed by Smartmatic—a UK based voting machinery company.

Lawsuit background: Smartmatic initiated a lawsuit in February 2021, claiming that the media giant spread false information about Smartmatic's involvement in election fraud during the 2020 election. The lawsuit alleges that the parent directed on-air talent that was repeatedly played on Fox News, calling it a "disinformation campaign" to regain viewership after Joe Biden's victory.

"Fox News Network's daily operations in connection with that coverage sufficiently allege Fox Corporation's "direct liability" for the challenged defamatory statements, as well as satisfy the applicable pleading requirements for alleging actual malice," the court said.

“We will be ready to defend this case surrounding extremely newsworthy events when it goes to trial,” Fox commented, according to a Reuters report. “As a report prepared by our financial expert shows, Smartmatic’s damages claims are implausible, disconnected from reality, and on their face intended to chill First Amendment freedoms.”

Previously, in 2023, Fox Corp. (FOX) paid more than $787 million to settle that lawsuit brought by Dominion Voting Systems.
